narrative: I'm sorry to share that your law school application was rejected because our predictive model estimates a 64.5% chance you'll fail the bar exam on your first try, based on patterns from thousands of past students. Your profile shows you ranked in the bottom decile for grades in both your first and third years of school, far below the typical student's middle-of-the-pack standing. Your LSAT score of 26 is also notably lower than the average of around 37, and your undergraduate GPA of 3.10 sits just a bit under the usual 3.21. Coming from an upper-middle-class family puts you in a solid income bracket compared to most, you're a full-time student like the vast majority, and as a Black male, your background is less common in the dataset where White students dominate at nearly 84%. The model's decision was driven mostly by your third-year school decile rank, which had the strongest pull toward predicting failure, followed closely by your low LSAT score. Your first-year decile rank came next in influence, also pushing the prediction toward failure, with family income and racial background playing supporting roles in that direction. Your undergraduate GPA added a moderate nudge toward failure, while your choice to study full-time had a tiny positive effect, and being male slightly favored a passing prediction. Overall, these academic markers—especially your low decile ranks and LSAT—stood out as the biggest factors compared to students who typically pass, who have stronger early and later school performances and higher test scores. To boost your future chances, focus on what you can realistically improve, like retaking the LSAT to aim for a score closer to or above the average, building a stronger academic record in any ongoing studies, and seeking targeted bar exam prep resources that have helped similar students succeed despite early setbacks. With dedication to these areas, you could shift the odds in your favor for another application down the line. Keep pushing—you've got potential, and many have turned things around from here.
